title: NOVA's Secret
tagline: She saw the full design before she came down. She didn't tell them everything. Now the time for that is over.
villain_mandate: RUMBLE IS NOT PRESENT this episode but his presence is felt in every scene. The city-scale device NOVA reveals must feel genuinely impossible to stop. RUMBLE's intelligence advantage must be stated explicitly: he has already anticipated their countermoves. The team must end this episode knowing what they're facing is bigger than anything before — and that RUMBLE has been ahead of them the entire time.

focus_hero.name: NOVA
focus_hero.role: THIS IS HER EPISODE. She held back information to prevent panic — now she reveals it. Her warmth comes through in the objects she builds, not words. This episode, the objects she builds are for a different purpose.
focus_hero.visual: Sleek orbital support mech, analysis screens, calm analytical glow — this episode she's on the ground with the team for the first time
focus_hero.character_note: NOVA doesn't explain why she withheld the information. She shows them instead. Understanding her choice is the emotional throughline.

hero_2.name: BLAZE
hero_2.role: First to understand what NOVA is showing them. Asks the right question. Doesn't like the answer.
hero_2.visual: Red-orange mech, fire-jets dim (processing mode, not fight mode)

hero_3.name: STORM
hero_3.role: Runs calculations while NOVA talks. Her speed is useful here — she's processing implications in real-time.
hero_3.visual: Silver-blue mech, speed lines subdued — this is listening mode

absent.name: GRANITE
absent.role: In repair. Reinforcement sleeve recharging. NOVA checks on him first before the briefing — the audience sees his status without him being part of the main scene.
absent.note: His absence is felt. The team is down one and facing their biggest threat.

threat.name: The RESONANCE TOWER
threat.description: RUMBLE's city-scale device. Uses the city's full power grid (which he stole the map for in EP005) to generate a resonance frequency that disables ALL mech systems simultaneously within a 10-kilometer radius. Not just makes movement hard — complete shutdown. Every mech in the city, offline at once. Permanently, if held long enough. RUMBLE would own the city in silence.
threat.construction_status: NOVA estimates 72 hours. But RUMBLE found a power source in 2 days instead of 2 weeks before. Her estimates have been wrong before.
threat.rumble_advantage: He already knows NOVA's countermeasure design. The blank sections in his schematics that NOVA identified? He left them blank intentionally — as a decoy. His real construction path is completely different.

scenes.title: NOVA Comes Down
scenes.narration: NOVA had been in orbit for eleven weeks before EP004. She had come down because the weapon's timeline accelerated. But there was a second reason she hadn't mentioned. The morning after the Gravity Anchor, she descended from orbit for the first time since her arrival. The team watched her land. She was smaller than they'd imagined. She said: 'I need to show you something. I should have shown you before I came down.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. Dawn over Metron City. NOVA's orbital form descends — a trail of blue-white light from the sky — and lands in the team's base for the first time. BLAZE and STORM watch her touch down. She's smaller than expected — precise, compact, analytical, not imposing. The team looks at her. She looks at them. Behind her, the dawn city skyline. She projects a small holographic display from her palm immediately — not a greeting, a purpose. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, dawn over Metron City, sleek precise support mech descending from orbit in trail of blue-white light and landing in team base for first time, compact and smaller than expected, red-orange mech and silver-blue mech watching her touch down, she immediately projects small holographic display without greeting, dawn city skyline behind her, purpose-first arrival, 16:9

scenes.title: She Checks on GRANITE First
scenes.narration: Before anything else, NOVA walked to the repair bay. GRANITE was there — reinforcement sleeve dark, systems at minimum. She examined him for thirty seconds. She adjusted two connections in the sleeve without being asked. Then she said: 'Seventy-two hours for full restoration. Possibly sixty if the secondary system holds.' She put her hand on his chassis for one second. Then she walked back to the main room. She didn't say anything else about it.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. QUIET CHARACTER MOMENT — NOVA stands beside GRANITE in the repair bay. He's offline at minimum power, reinforcement sleeve dark. She examines him clinically — runs a scan, adjusts two connector points in the sleeve with careful precision. Then she places her hand on his chassis for exactly one beat. It's not a speech. It's not a declaration. It's an action. Then she walks back. BLAZE watches from the doorway. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, quiet character moment — sleek precise mech standing beside massive offline grey mech in repair bay, examining clinically then adjusting two connection points in damaged sleeve with careful precision, then placing hand on chassis for one single beat of warmth without words, walking away, red-orange mech watching from doorway, repair bay amber lighting, 16:9

scenes.title: What She Found in the Schematics
scenes.narration: NOVA projected the RUMBLE schematic. Not the version she'd shown them before — the full version. The blank sections were highlighted. She said: 'When I identified these as the weapon design, I was partially right. The Gravity Anchor was in here. But these sections—' she highlighted a larger cluster, '—are not the Gravity Anchor. They're the power routing for something that uses the full city grid. I found this before I left orbit. I came down early because of it.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. NOVA projects the full RUMBLE schematic — large, complex, detailed. She highlights the Gravity Anchor section (already familiar). Then she highlights a MUCH LARGER cluster of blank sections. The scale comparison is immediate and alarming — the Gravity Anchor was one small section. This is the majority of the schematic. BLAZE and STORM stare at it. NOVA's expression: not afraid, but honest about what this means. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, support mech projecting full complex schematic hologram, highlighting small familiar section for gravity anchor then highlighting much larger cluster of sections — scale comparison immediately alarming as larger section dwarfs the first, red-orange mech and silver mech staring at it, support mech's expression honest about implications without fear, tense revelation, 16:9

scenes.title: The RESONANCE TOWER
scenes.narration: She showed them the full design. The RESONANCE TOWER — a city-scale device using Metron City's entire power grid to generate a frequency that would shut down every mech system in a ten-kilometer radius. Simultaneously. Completely. STORM ran the math in 4 seconds and said nothing for 3 more. BLAZE said: 'Every mech. That includes us.' NOVA said: 'Yes.' BLAZE said: 'And the city's emergency systems.' NOVA said: 'Yes.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. NOVA's display shows the RESONANCE TOWER design — a massive structure integrated into the city's power grid, radiating outward in a perfect circle over a 10-kilometer map of Metron City. The radius covers everything. STORM's visor shows calculation results — her expression after 4 seconds of math is the answer. BLAZE looks at the map. Looks at NOVA. The question is already asked. The answer is already given. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, full display of Resonance Tower design — massive structure with 10-kilometer radius circle over city map covering everything, silver mech's visor showing calculation results with grim expression after 4 seconds, red-orange mech looking at map then at support mech with the question that doesn't need asking, support mech answering yes to the questions being asked, weight of total scope, 16:9

scenes.title: He Already Knows Our Counter
scenes.narration: BLAZE asked the next question: 'Can we disrupt the tower the same way we disrupted the Gravity Anchor?' NOVA said: 'No.' She pulled up a secondary schematic. 'The blank sections I found — I thought they were the weapon. They were a decoy. He left them blank for me to find. His real construction path is here.' A completely different schematic appeared. 'He anticipated the resonance disruptor. He's already designed around it.' STORM said, very quietly: 'He's been watching us.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. NOVA swaps schematics — the original 'blank section' schematic REPLACED by a completely different construction pathway. The old one was the bait. This is the real weapon. BLAZE's fists tighten. STORM goes very still. NOVA's display shows side-by-side: the resonance disruptor frequency vs. the tower's counter-frequency design — RUMBLE built around their only known tool. The word DECOY is implied in every line of the display. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, support mech swapping holographic schematics — old 'blank section' diagram replaced by completely different real construction pathway, red-orange mech's fists tightening, silver mech going very still, display showing side-by-side resonance disruptor frequency vs tower's counter-frequency design meaning their only known tool is already defeated, RUMBLE three moves ahead, 16:9

scenes.title: Why She Didn't Say This Before
scenes.narration: BLAZE asked: 'Why didn't you tell us all of this when you arrived?' NOVA didn't answer immediately. She looked at the display. Then she said: 'I needed to see how you fought first. The Gravity Anchor battle gave me more information about RUMBLE's real patterns than eleven weeks of schematic analysis. I needed that data before I could design a counter that would actually work.' She paused. 'And I wasn't sure you could handle knowing the full scope on day one.' STORM said: 'Can we handle it now?' NOVA said: 'You have to.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. EMOTIONAL CENTER — NOVA facing BLAZE directly for the first time in the episode. She doesn't look apologetic — she looks honest. She explains her reasoning clinically: she needed combat data. Then her second reason, quieter. BLAZE processes both. It's not quite forgiveness, not quite anger — it's understanding something complicated. STORM watches them. The room is quiet. NOVA's display is still up, showing the full impossible scope of what they're facing. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, emotional center scene — precise support mech facing red-orange mech directly with honest not apologetic expression explaining her reasoning, leader processing something complicated that's not quite anger and not quite forgiveness, silver mech watching quietly, full impossible schematic still glowing in background, quiet honest difficult conversation, 16:9

scenes.title: What She's Been Building
scenes.narration: NOVA said: 'I didn't come down empty-handed.' She projected three new device designs — different from the EP004 devices, designed for what she now knew RUMBLE had built. 'These require components I don't have yet. And GRANITE needs to be operational.' She looked at the repair bay door. 'Sixty hours. Maybe fifty-eight.' BLAZE looked at the Resonance Tower schematic. NOVA had estimated seventy-two hours until completion. That number hadn't changed. The margin was thin.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. NOVA projects THREE NEW device designs — different from EP004's devices, purpose-built for the Resonance Tower. They're complex, clearly not finished. She projects a timeline comparison: 72 hours (RUMBLE's tower completion) vs 58-60 hours (team prep minimum). The margin is visible and insufficient. BLAZE stares at it. STORM calculates. The repair bay door is in the background. The clock is already running. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, support mech projecting three new device designs alongside timeline comparison showing 72 hours for tower completion vs 58-60 hours needed for team prep — margin visible and insufficient, red-orange mech staring at timeline, silver mech calculating, repair bay door visible in background implying absent teammate, devices complex and unfinished, clock already running, 16:9

scenes.title: What RUMBLE Is Doing Right Now
scenes.narration: Meanwhile. RUMBLE stood inside the construction site of the Resonance Tower — not a site they knew about, not a location in any city record. He looked at the power routing map he'd taken from the Metron City grid. He looked at his construction timeline. He crossed one line off his list. The line said: NOVA REVEALS SCOPE TO TEAM. He had anticipated this too. He added a new line: ADJUST TIMELINE. Then he wrote a number. The number was smaller than seventy-two hours.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. VILLAIN INSERT — RUMBLE at the Resonance Tower construction site, deep underground, city power lines visible in the walls. He holds the power grid map from EP005. He looks at a physical list — crossing off a line. We can see it says something about the team finding out. He writes a new number. We see it, but just barely — it's less than 72. He's already adjusted. He's already ahead. He doesn't look up. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, massive dark-metal mech at underground Resonance Tower construction site with city power lines visible in walls, holding power grid map, crossing off a line on physical list that references the team, writing new smaller timeline number with absolute calm, never looking up, not rushing, three moves ahead villain insert, ominous underground construction scale, 16:9

scenes.title: What They Do With It
scenes.narration: BLAZE looked at NOVA's devices. At GRANITE's repair bay. At STORM. At the timeline. He said: 'Tell us what you need.' NOVA said: 'Component list. I'll transmit it now.' STORM was already moving — her speed used for something new: sourcing. BLAZE stood at the display for a moment longer. The Resonance Tower schematic rotated slowly. He turned away from it. Not because it wasn't real. Because there was work to do.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. RESOLVE SHOT — BLAZE faces his team, fire-jets beginning to glow with purpose. NOVA transmits the component list — it appears on everyone's displays simultaneously. STORM is already at speed, beginning the sourcing run. The Resonance Tower schematic still rotates behind them. BLAZE turns away from it deliberately — not in denial, in purpose. The team is moving. The margin is 12 hours. The margin is all they have. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, resolve shot — red-orange mech with fire-jets beginning to glow with purpose, support mech transmitting component list to all displays simultaneously, silver mech already at speed beginning sourcing run, Resonance Tower schematic still rotating behind them, leader turning deliberately away from it in purpose not denial, team in motion, 16:9

scenes.title: GRANITE Wakes Up Early
scenes.narration: Fifty-one hours later — seven hours ahead of NOVA's estimate — GRANITE's systems came back online. His reinforcement sleeve activated. He sat up in the repair bay and looked at the component list on the display. He looked at the timeline. He looked at the door. He stood up. He said: 'What did I miss?' NOVA appeared in the doorway. She said: 'Good timing.' She handed him a device. Episode 7.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. CLIFFHANGER — GRANITE's repair bay. His systems coming back online — reinforcement sleeve activating with amber glow. He sits up, looks at the component list and timeline on the display above him. He reads it. He stands. His expression: he understands exactly what it means. NOVA appears in the doorway — she'd been waiting, watching the restoration process. She holds out one of the new devices. His hand closes around it. Ready. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, massive grey mech's systems coming back online in repair bay with reinforcement sleeve activating in amber glow, sitting up and reading component list and timeline display with expression of full comprehension, standing with resolve, sleek support mech in doorway having waited and watched the restoration, extending one of the new devices, mech's hand closing around it, ready, 16:9

music.notes: Scene 1: descending tone — something important arriving. Scene 2: very quiet, almost no music — this is a private moment. Scene 3-4: building dread as the scale of the threat is revealed. Scene 5: silence for 2 beats when NOVA says 'He anticipated the resonance disruptor.' Then the music returns darker. Scene 6: the quietest moment of the episode — let the conversation breathe, no hero swells. Scene 7: analytical, ticking quality — time is running. Scene 8: RUMBLE's theme, low and satisfied. Scene 9: purpose builds (not triumphant, not desperate — focused). Scene 10: ending on hope — GRANITE's activation theme, then quiet.

voice_style.pacing: The revelations in scenes 3-5 should come fast — NOVA delivers information without softening it. Scene 6 slows completely. 'You have to.' should land as a full stop. Scene 8 (RUMBLE insert) is slow and quiet — his confidence doesn't need speed.
voice_style.character: A narrator who wants the team to succeed and is genuinely unsure they have enough time

nova_character_note: NOVA's warmth is never stated, always shown. Scene 2: she adjusts GRANITE's sleeve without being asked. Scene 6: she held back information to gather better data — this is her version of protecting the team. Scene 10: she was waiting in the doorway. She knew when he'd wake up. She had the device ready.
rumble_wins_audit: RUMBLE doesn't appear until scene 8. His presence dominates the entire episode. He: (1) anticipated NOVA's reveal, (2) deliberately planted a decoy schematic for her to find, (3) has already adjusted his timeline, (4) is further along than NOVA estimated. He hasn't made a mistake yet. The team is reacting to him. He is not reacting to them.
youtube_title: NOVA's Been Keeping a SECRET This Whole Time... 😰 | Mech Legends Episode 6
youtube_description:
She came down from orbit with everything the team needed.

Except one thing.

The full truth.

In today's episode:
🔵 NOVA lands on the ground for the first time
🔴 The RESONANCE TOWER — RUMBLE's real weapon, city-scale
😱 He already knew our counter. He PLANNED for it.
💎 GRANITE wakes up early
⏱️ The clock is running. The margin is 12 hours.

📺 Watch ALL Mech Legends episodes: [playlist link]
🔔 SUBSCRIBE — Episode 7 next week!

#MechLegends #NOVA #RUMBLE #ResonanceTower #KidsAction #MechCartoon
next_episode_preview: Episode 7 — The Component Run. They need four parts from four locations. RUMBLE knows they're coming. He's already there.
higgsfield_style_notes: NOVA's design should feel precise and compact — she's not imposing, she's precise. Her holographic displays should be the most visually sophisticated thing in the episode — clean blues and golds, data-dense but readable. The Resonance Tower schematic should feel genuinely large and city-spanning — when the radius circle appears over the Metron City map, it should cover everything visible. RUMBLE's scene 8 insert should be lit differently from every other scene — darker, underground, power cables in the walls.
